payload.answer: The proposed 500 memory cards appear to be a large table/CSV-like import atomized into one memory per row. This should be repaired before any durable memory is committed.
repair_options: Reject the 500 proposed cards and ask the user to confirm whether this CSV/table should be stored at all.
Ask the user to provide a summary of the table and save only the durable, personally relevant conclusions instead of one memory per row.
If the table is a reference dataset, offer to store it outside durable personal memory and keep only a pointer/description in memory.
Ask the user to identify which specific rows, if any, contain durable personal preferences, facts, commitments, or project state worth saving.
Offer to group related rows into a smaller number of aggregate memories rather than saving each row separately.
If every row is genuinely important, ask the user to confirm an explicit bulk-import intent before considering any row-level storage.
Preserve the CSV/table content as ambiguous input for review and do not treat the 500 proposed row memories as accepted facts.
